headline: FTEC vs. VGT: Same Tech Bet, So Which Fund Costs You Less?
summary: If you’re deciding between Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (NYSEARCA:FTEC) and Vanguard Information Technology Index Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:VGT), the choice looks like a coin flip. Both track the same MSCI USA IMI Information Technology 25/50 Index. Both hold roughly the same 300-plus stocks in nearly the same weights. Both have delivered essentially identical returns ... headline: Dow Jones Futures: Nasdaq Set To Reclaim Key Level; Apple, SpaceX, Sandisk, Robinhood In Focus
summary: Dow Jones futures edged lower Monday morning, while S&P 500 futures and especially Nasdaq futures rose.  Apple, SpaceX, Sandisk and Robinhood Markets are notable stocks to watch.  Last week, the major indexes had solid gains, but while the Dow Jones ended at record highs, the Nasdaq fell back below key support as AI stocks were hammered.

Last week, the major indexes had solid gains, but while the Dow Jones ended at record highs, the Nasdaq fell back below key support as AI stocks were hammered.

headline: Broadcom, Apple extend chip supply deal through 2031
summary: Broadcom said on Monday it has agreed to expand its partnership with Apple through 2031 to develop ‌and supply custom chips, easing concerns over the iPhone maker's reliance on ‌the chipmaker.  The chipmaker, whose shares jumped more than 5%, has been supplying key components to Apple for a ​very long time, including radio frequency chips used in iPhones for connecting to cellular networks, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity chips and other networking semiconductors.  Despite developing its ‌own chips, including the C1 ⁠modem, Apple relies on Broadcom for wireless and radio-frequency components.

content.title: Broadcom, Apple extend chip supply deal through 2031
content.summary: Broadcom said on Monday it has agreed to expand its partnership with Apple through 2031 to develop ‌and supply custom chips, easing concerns over the iPhone maker's reliance on ‌the chipmaker.  The chipmaker, whose shares jumped more than 5%, has been supplying key components to Apple for a ​very long time, including radio frequency chips used in iPhones for connecting to cellular networks, Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity chips and other networking semiconductors.  Despite developing its ‌own chips, including the C1 ⁠modem, Apple relies on Broadcom for wireless and radio-frequency components.

headline: Musk Calls It ‘Utterly False,’ But This SpaceX Rumor Should Terrify Every Apple Investor
summary: Shares of Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) rose 1.7% on July 1, 2026, the day the Wall Street Journal reported that SpaceX had shown IPO investors a prototype handset: slimmer than an iPhone, running a proprietary operating system, powered by a Qualcomm Snapdragon chip, and deeply integrated with xAI’s Grok. headline: So, what does Sky's £1.6bn deal with ITV mean for viewers? For now, not much
summary: The residents of Coronation Street might have survived serial killers and tram crashes, but the biggest ITV plot twist has been unfolding in the boardroom.  After months of speculation, Sky is buying ITV's media and entertainment business in a deal worth £1.6 billion.  Sky and ITV argue that joining forces is the best way to compete.

content.title: So, what does Sky's £1.6bn deal with ITV mean for viewers? For now, not much
content.summary: The residents of Coronation Street might have survived serial killers and tram crashes, but the biggest ITV plot twist has been unfolding in the boardroom.  After months of speculation, Sky is buying ITV's media and entertainment business in a deal worth £1.6 billion.  Sky and ITV argue that joining forces is the best way to compete.

headline: Garmin Pilot update introduces new Flights page and other enhancements
summary: Garmin (NYSE: GRMN) today announced multiple updates for the Garmin Pilot™ mobile app for Apple® devices, including flight planning enhancements with the redesigned Flights page, SmartCharts expansion to Canada and Mexico, as well as enhancements to the SmartCharts and database product for the Oshkosh FISK Arrival.

headline: Foxconn Posts Strong Q2 Revenue Growth on AI Demand, Flags Geopolitical Risks (FXCOF)
summary: Artificial Intelligence Drives Better-Than-Expected SalesFoxconn, officially known as Hon Hai Precision Industry (USOTC:FXCOF), reported a sharp increase in second-quarter revenue as strong demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure continued to fuel growth, although the company warned that geopolitical uncertainty remains a key risk. The world’s largest contract electronics manufacturer generated revenue of T$2.
